title: Radar Sub-System Maintenance and Modification
entry_mechanism: Progressive capability build from line maintenance to depot-level repair under OEM licence
executive_summary:
Naval radar systems — air search, surface search, fire control, and navigation radars aboard Saudi corvettes and frigates — are among the most technically complex and strategically sensitive systems on a warship. Today, any radar failure beyond basic line maintenance (replacement of front-line-replaceable units) requires the system to be shipped to the OEM facility in Europe for depot-level repair — a process that takes weeks to months and leaves the vessel without critical sensor capability.

Node NV-5 targets a progressive build from line maintenance (what Saudi technicians can do today) to depot-level repair (what they will do by Phase II end) and eventually modification authority (Phase III aspiration). The progression follows a structured three-stage model: Stage 1 — Saudi technicians perform scheduled maintenance and LRU replacement under OEM supervision; Stage 2 — Saudi technicians perform component-level repair of non-classified sub-assemblies; Stage 3 — Saudi engineers perform modification and upgrade under OEM licence.

The radar systems on the Avante 2200 are primarily European-origin (non-ITAR) — Indra's SPS-N 5000 air search radar, Hensoldt navigation radar — making them accessible for in-Kingdom maintenance development without US export control restrictions.

companies.name: Indra
companies.rationale: Indra supplies the SPS-N 5000 radar on the Avante 2200 and has Saudi Arabia maintenance obligations. Indra's depot repair licensing for Saudi technicians is the natural extension of the corvette programme.
companies.contact: Indra VP Naval Electronic Systems, After-Sales

companies.name: Thales Naval
companies.rationale: Thales supplies naval radar to regional navies and has an active Gulf support presence. Thales's SMART-S and Sea Master radar families have established maintenance programmes.
companies.contact: Thales Naval VP After-Sales, Middle East

companies.name: Hensoldt
companies.rationale: Hensoldt supplies navigation and fire control radar to the Saudi corvette programme and has a track record of international maintenance licensing.

companies.name: Leonardo (formerly Selex)
companies.rationale: Leonardo supplies naval radar to multiple Gulf navies and has established regional maintenance partnerships.

companies.name: Saab (Gothenburg)
companies.rationale: Saab has naval radar and electronics maintenance capabilities and active Gulf navy relationships through the Royal Saudi Naval Forces.

partners.name: SAMI Electronics / Naval Electronics Division
partners.current: Some electronics maintenance; communications equipment repair; limited radar line maintenance
partners.upgrade: Establish naval radar maintenance shop; Indra depot maintenance licence; radar test equipment procurement; 20 Saudi radar technicians trained at Indra Madrid facility

partners.name: KACST Electronics Research
partners.current: Signal processing research; RF and microwave engineering; some radar research
partners.upgrade: Naval radar signal processing research relevant to maintenance diagnostics; Saudi radar test procedures development; support Saudi radar engineer training

partners.name: Saudi Aramco Communications Division
partners.current: Microwave and satellite communications maintenance; some radar-adjacent systems
partners.upgrade: Extend RF maintenance capability to naval radar frequency bands; dual-use test equipment investment; Saudi RF technician pipeline

partners.name: KFUPM Electrical Engineering / RF Lab
partners.current: RF and microwave engineering research; antenna theory; some radar systems courses
partners.upgrade: Naval radar engineering programme; Indra-sponsored laboratory equipment; graduate placement at SAMI naval electronics division
